sources_exist.note: All 11 evidence_facts[].url occurrences resolved live. The Q1 2026 OPTIS PDF resolved through the download link on its official OPTIS landing page to the exact recorded URL.

figures_match_sources.note: Six of 11 atomic facts pass exact quote, title, attribution, and scope review. F5 changes the PDF's quotation-mark characters, F6 falsely reports geography as not reported, F7 records a section heading instead of the live page title, and F8/F11 falsely report geography as not reported despite the guide's explicit U.S. state-regulatory context.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The exact ESTIMATE_HEAVY flag was audited across all ten named estimates. current_adoption_pct, t50_years, owners_60plus_pct, pi_moat, and pricing_structure are candid estimates with ranges, but ai_replaceable_share uses licensing as a task-automation constraint, pi_dist misuses F9, historical_analogs omits its search basis, and buy_mult/exit_mult rely only on a stale broad-market range without target-tier or owner-compensation support. The two unflagged OBSERVED packets also hide material population bridges.

rubric_consistent.note: Identity, v3.1.1 schema, deterministic dataset injection, role rows/residual, provenance mapping, and all Python arithmetic recompute exactly. The record nevertheless violates frozen research rules by using F9 M&A/ownership-change evidence in C and F4 licensing evidence to constrain V, and it fails to disclose sensitivity to the n_band margin proxy.

cross_checks_honest.note: Uplift timing and PRESSURED terminal logic are internally coherent, but no_proxy_confirmed is false because F9 M&A evidence feeds pi_dist/C, and dataset_mismatch.applies=false omits the deterministic n_band's Insurance (General) margin proxy and associated normalization sensitivity.

source_audits.note: F1's title and exact 8% embedded-workflow figure appear on the live February 19, 2026 Big I page; the page also identifies the national email survey of independent agency members of various sizes, supporting the recorded population, U.S. context, unit, period, and disclosed mismatch.

F11's contiguous direct-online and commission-cost quote, title, date, population, and unit are supported, but scope.geography='not reported' is false: the guide expressly anchors licensing in a U.S. state system and describes NAIC as the U.S. standard-setting organization.

F2's title and exact 22% limited-area-use figure appear contiguously on the live page, and the survey population, U.S. context, respondent-percent unit, period, and disclosed industry mismatch are accurate.

F3's title and exact 60% operational-efficiency expected-benefit figure appear contiguously on the live page; the recorded respondent population, U.S. context, expected-benefit unit, period, and labor-reduction mismatch are accurate.

F4's Producer Licensing title, exact contiguous licensing sentence, February 10, 2025 update date, U.S. geography, covered activities and people, unit, and firm-level mismatch all match the live NAIC page.

The exact PDF resolves and supports 139 distinct buyers over eight quarters and 38% (53) with four or more deals in the stated North American agent-and-broker context, but the recorded quote is not exact: the PDF prints curly quotation marks around “Active Buyers,” while F5 substitutes straight quotation marks.

The exact contiguous over-30,000/no-ability-to-perpetuate sentence, title, source population, unit, and January 20, 2026 publication context are supported. The scope fails because geography='not reported' contradicts the PDF cover's explicit 'North American Agent & Broker' title.

The live MarshBerry page supports the exact broad mid-single-digit-to-mid-teens EBITDA-multiple sentence, publication date, population, unit, and disclosed target mismatch. It does not support the recorded source title: the live H1 is 'How to Calculate Your Insurance Firm’s Value'; F7 instead copies a lower-page section heading.

F8's title, contiguous insurer-paid-commission quote, publication date, agent population, compensation-model unit, and industry mismatch are supported. The geography field is not: the article explicitly operates in a U.S. state-licensing and U.S. regulatory context, so 'not reported' is inaccurate.

F9's one-in-three ownership-change sentence appears contiguously on the live September 26, 2024 page; the U.S. independent P&C agency population, share unit, period, and narrower-than-NAICS mismatch are accurately stated.

F10's 87.7% commercial-lines written-premium figure and 87.9% 2024 comparison appear contiguously; the live page confirms the title, June 23, 2026 date, 2025 U.S. P&C premium data, channel-share unit, and disclosed market-channel mismatch.

reasons: evidence_facts[4].quote is not an exact copy of the PDF: the source uses curly quotation marks around “Active Buyers,” while the record uses straight quotation marks. Copy the exact contiguous PDF text without changing punctuation.
evidence_facts[5].scope.geography says 'not reported', but the cited PDF identifies its scope as North American in the cover title. Correct the geography to the source-reported scope.
evidence_facts[6].title is not the live source title: it copies a section heading, while the page H1 is 'How to Calculate Your Insurance Firm’s Value'. Record the exact page title.

evidence_facts[7].scope.geography and evidence_facts[10].scope.geography say 'not reported', but the NAIC guide explicitly places its advice in a U.S. state-licensing and U.S. regulatory context. Replace both fields with the source-supported geography.

inputs.active_consolidators is incorrectly OBSERVED: F5's 53 counts North American agent-and-broker buyers over eight quarters and the report includes wholesale and TPA activity, so using 53 for U.S. NAICS 524210 is a population bridge. Use ESTIMATE with the complete geography/industry mapping or cite an exact target-population count.

inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented is incorrectly OBSERVED for the scored population: F6 concerns independent agencies under $1.25 million revenue, not the constructed $1-10 million EBITDA band or all U.S. NAICS 524210 firms. A true selection requires a directly observed shortage in the applicable population, not this undisclosed population transfer.

inputs.pi_dist.estimate_basis.bridge_steps[2] both attributes 'carrier pressure' to F9 when its atomic quote states only M&A, perpetuation and ownership change, and uses M&A evidence in C even though the frozen rule permits M&A activity only in B and M. Remove F9 from pi_dist or add a separate atomic non-M&A pricing/bargaining fact and reassess the estimate.

inputs.ai_replaceable_share.estimate_basis.bridge_steps[1] uses F4's licensing boundary to separate automatable from retained tasks in V. The frozen rule says a statutory human-in-the-loop boundary does not make tasks non-automatable; rebuild the role judgments from task mechanics and reserve licensing for paid-demand/terminal or moat analysis.

inputs.historical_analogs.value asserts that no quantified historical analog was found, but its estimate_basis discloses no searches or candidate analogs examined. Enumerate the search basis and the absence judgment, or remove the unsupported search-coverage claim.

inputs.buy_mult and inputs.exit_mult rely only on an April 2022 broad brokerage-market range with geography and firm size unspecified and no target-band transaction tier or market-rate owner-compensation normalization. Obtain current target-tier EV/EBITDA support and enumerate the normalization and entry-to-exit bridge before selecting these multiples.

disclosures.dataset_mismatch.applies=false omits a material deterministic proxy: dataset_inputs.n_band uses a Damodaran 'Insurance (General)' 23.57% margin to classify insurance agencies/brokerages and does not establish target-band market-rate owner compensation. Disclose that proxy and provide a margin/normalization sensitivity; cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed must also acknowledge that F9 currently feeds C.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 10 inputs with provenance_type ESTIMATE (ai_replaceable_share, buy_mult, current_adoption_pct, exit_mult, historical_analogs, owners_60plus_pct, pi_dist, pi_moat, pricing_structure, t50_years)
